title: Oh Brother Where Bart Thou

jokes.text: Every gumdrop is made of a succulent substance called gelatin... Gelatin comes from the skin, bones and hooves of only the sickest horses.
jokes.setup: Educational TV show about candy making, starting with appetizing description
jokes.punchline: Reveals disgusting source material - 'sickest horses'
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection from appetizing to disgusting, good economy but fairly predictable gross-out humor

jokes.text: That's amazing. Change it! Change it! You just increased the volume!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to change channel from disgusting TV show
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer incompetence - can't operate remote properly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment but very familiar territory for the show

jokes.text: In Outstanding Lighting Direction, Electronic, Multi-camera for Variety, Music or Comedy Programming, we have... a three-way tie!
jokes.setup: Switching to awards show with incredibly specific category
jokes.punchline: Absurdly technical Emmy category that no one cares about
jokes.explanation: Good satire of overly specific Emmy categories but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Back to the horses!
jokes.setup: Homer prefers the disgusting horse gelatin show to boring awards
jokes.punchline: Chooses gross content over prestige TV
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character consistency - Homer would absolutely prefer gross content

jokes.text: Gee, Lisa, looks like tomorrow I'll be shoveling ten feet of global warming.
jokes.setup: Heavy snowfall outside
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically dismisses global warming with obvious contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment and topical humor, though the climate change confusion is well-worn territory

jokes.text: Well, aren't you the Queen of Crazyland? Everything's the opposite of everything.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains weather extremes
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive non-logic and made-up title
jokes.explanation: Strong character writing - 'Queen of Crazyland' is a perfect Homer-ism with good quotability

jokes.text: I'm Lisa Simpson. Really? Really? Uh-huh, all right.
jokes.setup: Lisa introduces herself proudly
jokes.punchline: Nelson's completely unimpressed, dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Nelson's indifference to Lisa's reputation

jokes.text: Mush, nerds, mush! I'm part of a team!
jokes.setup: Martin being pulled on sled by other nerds
jokes.punchline: Martin's delusional pride in being exploited
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Martin's oblivious enthusiasm for his own exploitation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Springfield Element... ...al Center for Periodic Table Studies.
jokes.setup: Second fake-out starting with 'Springfield Element...'
jokes.punchline: Another misdirection - chemistry school not elementary
jokes.explanation: Building the pattern but less clever than the first iteration

jokes.text: Springfield Elementary Sc... ...uba Diving Academy. Oh, for crying out loud!
jokes.setup: Third fake-out with 'Springfield Elementary Sc...'
jokes.punchline: Scuba diving academy + Homer's frustrated reaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag with Homer's mounting frustration adding to it

jokes.text: And of course, Springfield Elementary is closed. We were just messing with Bart Simpson.
jokes.setup: The whole fake-out sequence
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was intentionally targeting Bart specifically
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the running gag - reveals personal motivation and breaks fourth wall in character for Simpsons

jokes.text: Skinner! What factory makes just one kind of pasta?
jokes.setup: Skinner's nonsensical factory name
jokes.punchline: Logical criticism of his poor joke attempt
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that highlights the absurdity of Skinner's attempt

jokes.text: Uh, well, I just thought, uh, specialization being the wave of the future? Uh, probably, uh...
jokes.setup: Skinner being called out for his bad joke
jokes.punchline: His rambling, pseudo-intellectual defense
jokes.explanation: Excellent Skinner characterization - his pretentious scrambling when caught being silly

jokes.text: Really? Does Obama know about this?
jokes.setup: Homer learning DVDs run on electricity
jokes.punchline: His bizarre non-sequitur about Obama
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic - assumes the President should know about basic technology. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: This announcer has never seen diapers so dapper.
jokes.setup: Fashion show commentary on Maggie's outfit
jokes.punchline: Alliterative description treating diaper as haute couture
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but fairly predictable fashion show parody

jokes.text: Hello Kitty? I thought they were Spider-Man heads.
jokes.setup: Bart wearing girls' tights thinking they're masculine
jokes.punchline: His mortification at the girly reality
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - his masculine delusion meeting feminine reality

jokes.text: I don't need a brother. I'm a bad ass loner like Wolverine, who leaves whenever people beg him not to leave.
jokes.setup: Bart defending his brotherless status
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and backwards Wolverine reference
jokes.explanation: Good character writing - Bart's masculine posturing with confused pop culture logic

jokes.text: You've got the bike. It's not the same.
jokes.setup: Bart wanting a turn on something in dream
jokes.punchline: The classic sibling sharing conflict
jokes.explanation: Familiar sibling dynamic but serves the plot well

jokes.text: I'm the oldest!
jokes.setup: Brothers talking about achievements
jokes.punchline: Cooper's petulant assertion of birth order supremacy
jokes.explanation: Good sibling rivalry moment with real Manning brothers reference

jokes.text: I don't know why they say you're the dumb guy, Tom.
jokes.setup: Smothers Brothers routine in Bart's dream
jokes.punchline: Dick calling Tom the dumb one while being confused himself
jokes.explanation: Good reference to the actual Smothers Brothers dynamic

jokes.text: You can have mine, but he's kind of an idiot.
jokes.setup: Bart wanting a baby brother
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's casual dismissal of his own brother
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse moment - his matter-of-fact delivery of harsh family truth

jokes.text: Point and shoot.
jokes.setup: Bart saying Homer never explained how his body works
jokes.punchline: Homer's hilariously inadequate sex education
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer moment - perfectly captures his crude simplicity and terrible parenting in two words

jokes.text: Maybe you could trick your parents into making a baby the way my Mom nearly tricked Charles Barkley.
jokes.setup: Milhouse suggesting trickery for baby-making
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific reference to his mom and Charles Barkley
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird and specific reference that creates so many disturbing implications in few words

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I killed Kenny! Ralph. No, I killed Kenny yesterday. What did I do now?
jokes.setup: Ralph having gaming mishap
jokes.punchline: South Park reference combined with Ralph's innocent confusion
jokes.explanation: Good meta-reference with Ralph's characteristic innocent confusion

jokes.text: You had me at 'five-course,' you lost me at 'romantic,' and you got me back at 'dinner.'
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Bart's romantic dinner description
jokes.punchline: His emotional journey through the sentence focused only on food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic and structure - his food obsession overriding romantic concerns

jokes.text: There comes a time in every marriage when it comes down to this. Do we eat dessert, or do we make love?
jokes.setup: Homer faced with dessert and implied romance
jokes.punchline: His framing it as an either/or choice
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor with Homer's characteristic prioritization issues

jokes.text: We couldn't do both. No... we could not.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting they could do both dessert and romance
jokes.punchline: Their defeated admission of limitation
jokes.explanation: Good married couple humor about diminished expectations

jokes.text: Hey, this is the DVD my parents used to make me.
jokes.setup: Nelson providing educational sex DVD
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing disturbing family information
jokes.explanation: Excellent Nelson characterization - his casual delivery of deeply inappropriate family details

jokes.text: People in other countries make love?
jokes.setup: Homer seeing foreign instructional video
jokes.punchline: His shocked discovery about international sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good Homer ignorance moment - his provincial worldview about basic human behavior

jokes.text: The Yawning Monkey? The Pair Of Tongs? Ooh, look. Congress Of The Crow!
jokes.setup: Consulting an instructional video about positions
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous names for sexual positions
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist humor with memorable made-up position names.

jokes.text: More Vicodin and eggs, please.
jokes.setup: The previous scene's physical comedy
jokes.punchline: Homer's breakfast request suggesting injury and drug use
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - implies the entire aftermath in five words. Dark but efficient.

jokes.text: Mine do it once a year on the magical day when the prison and the insane asylum have their annual mixer.
jokes.setup: Nelson explaining his parents' relationship
jokes.punchline: Reveals both parents are institutionalized
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor perfectly suited to Nelson's character - explains his behavior while being genuinely shocking

jokes.text: Can't. Carpal-nurple syndrome. The doctor said if I didn't take it easy, I might never shove a kid's face in the drinking fountain again.
jokes.setup: Jimbo can't give wedgie due to injury
jokes.punchline: Medical condition from too much bullying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - 'carpal-nurple syndrome' is perfect wordplay and the medical concern is hilariously specific

jokes.text: I just wanted a baby brother, but Dad said I was one 'Uday' who didn't need a 'Qusay.'
jokes.setup: Bart explaining Homer's rejection
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate Hussein brothers reference
jokes.explanation: Clever dark wordplay that's perfectly inappropriate for Homer to say to his son

jokes.text: Shopping: it's the most fun you could have standing up.
jokes.setup: Discussion about shopping enjoyment
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about horizontal activities
jokes.explanation: Typical adult humor for the character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, you think he's for me! You know, the orphan is for my folks.. they can't have more kids. It is so sad... a real-life Jaws bit off my dad's wiener.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to adopt a child
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous lie about Homer's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bart's desperate lying with appropriately crude imagery

jokes.text: He could be the race car in Monopoly!
jokes.setup: Bart listing benefits for his brother
jokes.punchline: The ultimate sacrifice - best Monopoly piece
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid logic - offering what he himself values most

jokes.text: Favorite Beatle? Dung. Me, too!
jokes.setup: Bart asking about favorite Beatle
jokes.punchline: Charlie's misunderstanding and Bart's enthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with Bart's characteristic eagerness to bond

jokes.text: Uh, he was born with two noses and we hid him away till we could afford to cut one off.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why no one's seen Charlie
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous medical explanation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist lie that's weird enough to be believed by Simpsons characters

jokes.text: Two noses! You deserve a brother, Bart. You deserve good things!
jokes.setup: Bart's lie about Charlie's condition
jokes.punchline: Homer's emotional response to the fake medical issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely buying the lie and getting overly emotional.

jokes.text: You know, just like Annie, except he's a dude and he hates tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining Charlie is an orphan
jokes.punchline: Annie reference with masculine twist on optimism
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that inverts the musical's famous optimism

jokes.text: Be cool, be cool... he's an orphan. You know, just like Annie, except he's a dude and he hates tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Lisa discovering the truth about Charlie
jokes.punchline: Bart's pop culture comparison with the opposite personality trait
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of the Annie character with good specificity.

jokes.text: Awesome, they made a sequel to Sever IV.
jokes.setup: Bart seeing movie poster
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous horror movie franchise naming
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of horror movie sequel numbering

jokes.text: ...and murder and swears and brains and basements and bones and saws and lungs and faces worn as hats.
jokes.setup: Bart describing the violent movie to the ticket seller
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific and disturbing list ending with 'faces worn as hats'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that builds to an unexpectedly specific and disturbing climax.

jokes.text: Do not give him any more love. I repeat, do not give him any more love.
jokes.setup: Social worker retrieving orphan
jokes.punchline: Treating love like contraband
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist concept treating emotional connection as dangerous substance

jokes.text: How could you not see me? My arms are like hams. Hams!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy being knocked over
jokes.punchline: His outraged self-description using food metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - his pompous delivery of self-aware body description

jokes.text: The same place Eskimos hide from penguins.
jokes.setup: Where to hide in snow
jokes.punchline: Geographically impossible scenario
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist geography humor though not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Snow... God's cotton candy.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking what they'll eat in hiding
jokes.punchline: Bart's poetic but impractical description of snow
jokes.explanation: Beautiful poetic metaphor that shows Bart's romantic but impractical thinking.

jokes.text: Point and shoot, bro, point and shoot.
jokes.setup: Bart teaching Charlie what Homer taught him
jokes.punchline: Passing on terrible advice as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows the cycle of poor parenting with perfect economy

jokes.text: Now he has six wonderful sisters. He's so cute. Let's do his nails. He's my baby. He's my baby.
jokes.setup: Charlie getting adopted by loving family
jokes.punchline: His nightmare of being feminized by multiple sisters
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist on the 'happy ending' but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: I didn't think I heard what you think you said. Well, what do you think I said? I heard... It sounded like you said 'naked bacon.' That's what I thought. We all thought it was 'naked bacon.'
jokes.setup: The brothers having a communication breakdown
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous mishearing that everyone agrees on
jokes.explanation: Extended absurdist bit that captures the Smothers Brothers' style but runs a bit long.

jokes.text: I didn't, I didn't think I heard what you think you said... It sounded like you said 'naked bacon.'
jokes.setup: Smothers Brothers confusion routine
jokes.punchline: Mishearing creates absurd food phrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Smothers Brothers style wordplay confusion routine

tmdb_overview: One snowy day in Springfield, Lisa informs Bart that she and Maggie share a bond that Bart will never understand because he doesn't have a brother, so Bart asks Homer for a baby brother. When Homer denies Bart's request, Bart makes his way to the Springfield Orphanage to find what he thinks he's missing.
